mnemonics: AI PARENTS ML: ML is a child of AI, birthed from Statistics (its DNA), raised on Computational power (its food), and learns from Data (its experiences).
keyPoints: Machine Learning is a primary subset of Artificial Intelligence focused on systems that learn from data.
It has deep roots in Statistics, which provides the theoretical foundation for learning from data.
It relies on Computer Science for the algorithms and computational power needed to implement learning models efficiently.
commonMistakes: Confusing AI and ML: AI is the broader field of creating intelligent agents; ML is a specific approach to achieving it.
Overlooking the importance of statistics, seeing ML as purely a computer science discipline.
Assuming more data is always the solution, ignoring the need for quality data and proper statistical design.
